history.text: How did the west do last quarter?
"Last quarter" and "how did the west do" are both a bit underspecified, so let me look at what's available and check a query before I commit.
[call describe_manifold {}]
[tool describe_manifold -> 6 measures]

Two things I need to sort out before serving:

1. **"How did the west do"** — I can report a headline set of transaction metrics for the west (revenue, orders, buyers). Since a WHERE filter on region isn't supported, I'll put region in the anchor and we read the west row.

2. **"Last quarter"** — this is ambiguous. I don't have a "current date," so I can't infer which quarter you mean. The data covers a fixed span; I should show quarters and label which one we read.

Let me check the span and dry-run a query.
[call explain {"frameql": "SELECT revenue, orders, buyers AT {region*cal.quarter}"}]
[tool explain -> would-be outcome disclose]

The query is well-formed and would serve. But before I run it, one thing needs your call:

**"Last quarter" is ambiguous** — I don't have a current-date reference, and the recorded data spans multiple quarters (the calendar covers 2024–2025). I can't pick which quarter you mean on your behalf.

Rather than guess, here's what I propose: I'll serve **all quarters for the west** (revenue, orders, buyers), and you can read the specific quarter you have in mind from the labeled rows. If you tell me exactly which one (e.g. 2025-Q3), I'll pin it precisely.

Two folklore notes on the measures first:
- **revenue** — sale amount, summed.
- **orders** — count of sale lines.
- **buyers** — distinct customers who bought. Note: this is an **HLL distinct estimate** (~1.6% relative error), not an exact count.

Running the west's quarterly breakdown now — read the row for whichever quarter you meant:
[call query {"frameql": "SELECT revenue, orders, buyers AT {region*cal.quarter} ORDER BY region, cal.quarter"}]
engine: outcome=error; reason=unsupported; detail=sketch transport supports a single target level in this build. (shown to the human; you do not restate it)

The multi-level sketch transport for `buyers` failed. Let me reformulate once — I'll drop `buyers` from this juxtaposition so revenue and orders serve cleanly across the quarterly grain (buyers can be asked separately if you want it).
[call query {"frameql": "SELECT revenue, orders AT {region*cal.quarter} ORDER BY region, cal.quarter"}]
engine: outcome=serve; served columns [revenue, orders]. (values shown to the human)
West, Q4 2025.

That's the west's revenue and orders by quarter — the row you want is **west, 2025-Q4**.

Two notes:
- **"Last quarter"**: I couldn't infer a current date, so I served all quarters and you read 2025-Q4 (the latest in the data) as your "last quarter."
- I had to drop **buyers** (distinct customers) from this view — the engine can only transport that sketch to a single level at a time. If you'd like the west's buyer count too, just say so and I'll pull it on its own.

history.text: How are we doing on returns?
I'll look into the returns measure to understand what's available before proposing a query.
[call describe_measure {"measure": "units_returned"}]
[call describe_manifold {}]
[tool describe_measure -> units_returned on transaction]
[tool describe_manifold -> 6 measures]
"How are we doing on returns?" is broad. There's a `units_returned` measure and a declared `return_rate` derived (units_returned / units_sold). Let me check the time span so I can label it correctly.
[call explain {"frameql": "SELECT return_rate AS rate, units_returned AS returned, units_sold AS sold AT {cal.year}"}]
[tool explain -> would-be outcome serve]

This will serve cleanly. A note before the numbers on the two measures involved:

- **return_rate** — the declared definition is `units_returned / units_sold` (returned units over sold units), one definition for everyone.
- **units_returned** — units returned; **nulls predate returns tracking and count as no return**, so early periods may reflect that.

I'll break it out by year (the calendar covers 2024–2025) so you can see the trend rather than a single blended figure.
[call query {"frameql": "SELECT return_rate AS rate, units_returned AS returned, units_sold AS sold AT {cal.year}"}]
engine: outcome=serve; served columns [rate, returned, sold]. (values shown to the human)
By month, this year.

A note on the span: the label that came through says "this year," but the anchor I ran was `{cal.year}` and the recorded calendar covers **2024–2025 — so you're seeing both years, one row each**, not just this year.

That gives you the year-over-year picture on returns: the return rate (returned ÷ sold), plus the raw returned and sold counts behind it. If you'd like to go finer — by month to see the trend within a year, or by region or product to see where returns concentrate — just say which cut and I'll pull it.
